to form a committee.
Government of Maharashtra
Industries Energy, Labour and Mining Department
Government Decision No. MNG-0326 / P.No.15 / Industries-9B
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400 032.
Date: 25th March, 2026.
Read: -
1) Letter from the Director, Geology and Mining, Directorate, Nagpur
The Proposal: -
The Minister (Mines), Shri. As per the directions of the meeting held on 18.02.2026
Increase investment in the mining sector in the state, adoption of modern technology, mineral exploration
Maharashtra Minerals with the objective of promoting industrial growth and employment generation.
Maharashtra Mining Investment Conference to be held in Mumbai
is proposed. Strategic guidance, planning, and planning for the successful organization of the conference.
It is necessary to set up a high-level committee for coordination among various departments / institutions.
Since Ma. Formation of a Steering Committee under the Chairmanship of the Minister of State (Mines).
It was under the consideration of the government.
The Government's Decisions:
Strategic guidance for the successful conduct of the Maharashtra Mineral Investment Council,
The following are the high-level guidelines for planning and coordination among various departments / organizations:
A committee is being formed.
table_trans: Hon'ble Minister of State (Mines), Home (Rural), Housing, School Education and Cooperation.
Additional Chief Secretary (Mines), Industries, Power, Labour and Mining Department, Mantralaya, Mumbai.
Principal Secretary (Industries), Industries, Power, Labour and Mining Department, Mantralaya, Mumbai.
Deputy Director General, Indian Geological Survey (Maharashtra).
Chief Controller of Mines, Indian Bureau of Mines (IBM), Nagpur.
2 representatives of the Indian Mining Association.
Representatives of the Federation of Indian Mineral Industries
Director General, Directorate of Geology and Mines, Nagpur

para_trans: The rules of the Guiding Committee shall be as follows:
1. Provide overall policy guidance regarding the Council.
2. Plan, outline, format, schedule and date and time of the conference programme.
3. Coordination among various government departments, central agencies and other stakeholders.
4. To take necessary administrative and technical decisions for the Council.
5. Guidance from the Government of India to the Event Management Institute for organizing the Conference.
Selection and appointment as per instructions.
6. Selection and fixation of presentations and lecturers.
7. Deciding on other matters necessary for the successful conduct of the Conference.
3. Meetings of the Committee:
1. Meetings of the Committee shall be held with the approval of the Chairman, as the case may be.
The Committee shall have the power to invite experts as required.
This Government decision is available on the website of the Government of Maharashtra www.maharashtra.gov.in.
